title: Drug Use Disorder Education/Prevention - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Drug Use Disorder Education/Prevention 38 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ADDICTIONS RECOVERY CENTER OUTPATIENT TREATMENT AND MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient substance use disorder treatment, medication assisted treatment, problem gambling treatment, mental health counseling, and community education. 1025 E Main Street Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 779-1282 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HEALTH SERVICE BUILDING SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers individual and group counseling for people who want to change their relationship to substance use. Services may also include drug and alcohol assessments, drug screening, and case management. Peer support and recovery mentors are available, along with referrals to mental health and community resources. Also supports parent–child reunification, provides Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT), helps with admissions to detox and residential treatment, and creates relapse prevention plans to support long-term recovery. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday -Friday 8am-5pm (541) 766-6767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOYS & GIRLS CLUB OF WESTERN TREASURE VALLEY HEALTH AND FITNESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Focuses on improving the overall health of Club Members by increasing their daily physical activity through recreational activities and team sports. Offers a prevention and education program that addresses issues such as drug and alcohol use and premature sexual activity. 573 SW 3rd Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Ages 6-18 Hours: Monday-Friday 2:45pm-6:30pm on regular school days (follows the Ontario School District calendar), Summer: Monday-Friday 7:45am-5:30pm, Orientation: Wednesday 5:30pm Email (541) 889-7979 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADIA HEALTH CASCADIA PLAZA HEALTH CENTER CASCADIA CENTRAL INTAKE AND REFERRAL LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an intake line for services such as case management, individual, family, group, geriatric and LGBTQ counseling, alcohol and drug treatment services, DUII and gambling addiction/treatment services, recovery housing services, sexual abuse counseling, domestic violence services, urgent mental health services, medication management, money management, outreach, crisis intervention, outpatient mental health services, and early intervention services. Provides about 1000 units in sites operating as licensed residential, permanent supported housing, and independent housing. 4212 SE Division Street Suites 100 and 200 Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 674-7777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers alcohol and drug treatment services through a network of providers. Services also may be available for mandated treatment as the result of a DUII. Services include information and education, as well as treatment for substance use disorder issues. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 154 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Adults and youth with substance use issues Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 742-5335 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY COUNSELING SOLUTIONS ARLINGTON BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DRUG AND ALCOHOL TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works with individuals to overcome their addictions to alcohol and/or other drugs. Program involves complete alcohol and drug assessment, individual counseling, and classes for substance education and relapse prevention. 120 Arlington Mall Street Arlington, OR 97812 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Offers a variety of workshops that are suggested by participants such as sewing, harm reduction education, resume building, and more. Eligibility: Ages 16-24 Hours: Varies by program Email (541) 870-0036 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY ASSISTANCE AND RESOURCE CENTER GROUP NEEDLE EXCHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers outreach and harm-reduction services including a mobile needle exchange, education on safer drug use, testing services, and connections to detox and recovery programs. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm Email (541) 224-5538 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GIRLS INC OF THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST AFTER SCHOOL EMPOWERMENT PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-based empowerment programs and assertiveness education, including: -Allies in Action: Uses an interactive, peer-centered approach to reduce competition and hostility among girls -Friendly PEERsuasion: Addresses drug abuse prevention as a peer issue, encouraging young people to model healthy behaviors -Operation SMART: Mobilizes community resources to support girls -Economic Literacy: Guides girls in making sound financial decisions and building economic stability. These programs help girls feel more confident and capable in school by fostering self-esteem, collaborative learning, and other key developmental assets. 4800 S Macadam Avenue Suite 309 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Girls ages 6-18. Girls must attend the program associated with their school; participating schools vary. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 230-0054 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC PREVENTION AND RECOVERY TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides inpatient and outpatient substance use disorder treatment services, including assessment and individual and group treatment/education. If needed, services are coordinated with the local court system, SAFE Court, and Community Corrections to fulfill legal obligations. Other services include domestic violence intervention services, moral reconation groups, anger management, dual diagnosis treatment, and urinalysis and other screening tools. Substance use disorder prevention education available for children, parents, and community members. Also provides gambling assessment and treatment. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Assessment walk-in hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Office: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm, evening services available Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY ALCOHOL AND DRUG PREVENTION TREATMENT OUTPATIENT ALCOHOL, DRUG, AND GAMBLING TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prevention and treatment services for alcohol, drug, and gambling addiction. Treatment services include substance use disorder assessment and counseling, juvenile drug court treatment, gambling counseling, and referrals to residential treatment and other resources. Prevention services include education in schools and the community. 104 4th Avenue SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm. Evening treatment groups available Monday-Thursday. Other evening appointments by arrangement. Can also provide referrals to other agency services. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Ages 13-24 who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEXT DOOR YOUTH FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services, including counseling, support groups, violence and anger prevention training, life skills groups, parenting classes, new parenting groups, crime victims support, community information and referral and temporary housing. 965 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Ages 6-21 and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 386-6665 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST FAMILY SERVICES FAMILY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a wide range of services to individuals and families, including educational seminars and classes on a variety of topics. Programs focus on family stability and child well-being. Supports youth in their daily connections, emphasizing healthy decision-making. Areas of particular focus include drug, alcohol, and gang prevention as well as positive mental health strategies, including suicide prevention. 6200 SE King Road Portland, OR 97222 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 546-6377 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC KOLPIA COUNSELING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient substance use disorder counseling, mental health counseling, education, an integrative recovery program, and information about and referrals to Medically Assisted Treatment (MAT) services. Does not prescribe or dispense medication. 370 E Hersey Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: By appointment only, times vary (541) 482-1718 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTBACK STRONG ADULT SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER AND ADDICTIONS TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the following substance use disorder services: -Outpatient treatment and education for adolescents and adults -Individual, family, and group counseling options -DUII Diversion Treatment including DMV Certification -Substance abuse assessments and assist with referrals to inpatient treatment facilities if deemed necessary -Comprehensive prevention program -Anger management courses -Services related to gambling difficulties 215 N G Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 947-6021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTBACK STRONG CHRISTMAS VALLEY SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER AND ADDICTIONS TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides the following substance use disorder services: -Outpatient treatment and education for adolescents and adults -Individual, family, and group counseling options -DUII Diversion Treatment including DMV Certification -Substance abuse assessments and assist with referrals to inpatient treatment facilities if deemed necessary -Comprehensive prevention program -Anger management courses -Services related to gambling difficulties 87520 Bay Road Christmas Valley, OR 97641 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 947-6021 4 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX SAINTS LOVE LENTS COMMUNITY WELLNESS FAIR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hot meal and showers;
